title: Journey 1: Understanding Energy
goal: Students understand what energy is, how it changes form, how it moves through systems and why useful energy is often lost.
essentialQuestion: How does energy behave in natural and human-made systems?
challenge: Trace the energy changes in a device you use every day, then identify where useful energy appears and where energy is lost.

title: Journey 2: Energy in Natural Systems
goal: Students see how solar energy drives ecosystems and how energy flows through living systems.
essentialQuestion: How does energy enter and move through the natural world?
challenge: Create a short explanation showing how sunlight eventually becomes energy available to animals and humans.

title: Journey 3: Energy in Human Society
goal: Students understand how electricity and fuel systems support modern life through generation, grids and demand.
essentialQuestion: How do societies produce, distribute and use energy?
challenge: Choose one place such as a school, hospital or shopping centre and explain how it depends on multiple energy systems at once.

title: Journey 4: Energy and Environmental Impact
goal: Students connect energy systems to the carbon cycle, greenhouse effect and climate change.
essentialQuestion: Why do energy choices affect climate and sustainability?
challenge: Build a cause-and-effect chain that explains how burning fossil fuels contributes to climate change.

title: Journey 5: Renewable Energy and Future Systems
goal: Students explore cleaner energy options and understand why future systems need storage, smart grids and system-wide change.
essentialQuestion: What does a more sustainable energy system look like?
challenge: Design a more sustainable energy plan for a school or neighbourhood using at least three connected solutions.
